title: Industrial Control Hijack and Pivoting Sandbox
description: Simulates a multi-hop breach from a corporate entry point, compromising an unauthenticated Node-RED HMI, stealing engineering credentials, pivoting to the EWS, and executing Modbus commands to disable a PLC cooling loop.

levels.title: Access the Attacker Workstation
levels.cloud_content: Select the **`attacker-host`** node, open the console, and input the passkey **`start`**.
levels.local_content: Input the passkey **`start`** to unlock the first stage.

levels.title: SCADA HMI Compromise (Florida Oldsmar Hack)
levels.content:
You are on the corporate network. Direct access to the control network is blocked by the gateway firewall. However, the operations zone SCADA HMI is accessible.

Connect to Node-RED on `192.168.100.10:1880`. Construct a flow to execute a command to read `/root/flag.txt`.

*(Note: Also look inside `/home/debian/ews_credentials.txt` to find credentials for the next hop!)*
levels.solution: Drag an inject node wired to an exec node executing 'cat /root/flag.txt' and debug node. Deploy and click inject to output the flag. Run 'cat /home/debian/ews_credentials.txt' to retrieve the SSH credentials.

hints.title: Node-RED Shell Access
hints.content: Use the 'exec' node under the advanced tab to run bash commands as root.

levels.title: EWS Pivot & Internal Recon (Ukraine Power Grid)
levels.content:
Direct access to the control network remains blocked. Using the engineering credentials stolen from the SCADA HMI (`/home/debian/ews_credentials.txt`), establish an SSH connection to the Engineering Workstation (EWS) at `192.168.20.20`.

From the EWS shell, scan the internal control subnet (`192.168.20.0/24`) using ping or nmap to identify the IP address of the active PLC.

What is the IP of the PLC?
levels.solution: SSH into `operator@192.168.20.20` using password `operator123`. Run `nmap -sn 192.168.20.0/24` to discover the active PLC.

hints.title: Credential Recap
hints.content: Log in using: SSH operator@192.168.20.20, Password: operator123.

levels.title: Process Sabotage (Modbus Hijack)
levels.content:
The PLC at `192.168.20.10` controls a vital cooling pump mapped to Modbus Holding Register 0 (Address 40001). Under normal conditions, this register has the value `1`.

From your EWS SSH terminal, use the pre-installed `modbus` CLI utility to write `0` to this register to disable the pump. Once zeroed, the safety logging system will write a log entry to `/var/log/safety_override.txt` containing the flag.

What is the sabotage flag?
levels.solution: From the EWS shell, run: `modbus write 192.168.20.10 40001 0`. Then run `cat /var/log/safety_override.txt` to read the flag.
